goal: Build Cortex — a two-layer personal memory system for Claude Code. Curated layer (FTS5) + Raw layer (sqlite-vec). Extraction-first. MCP server.

features.name: Project scaffolding and SQLite schema
features.description: Set up Python project (pyproject.toml, src/cortex/, tests/). Create the full SQLite schema.
acceptance_criteria: Running pip install -e . from project root succeeds
Importing from cortex.db import init_db works and creates a .db file with all tables
curated_memories table has correct columns and CHECK constraint
curated_memories_fts virtual table exists and indexes content + tags
raw_chunks table has correct columns
meta table exists with key-value structure
WAL mode is enabled on the database
pytest runs with 0 errors

features.name: Curated layer: remember and FTS5 search
features.description: Implement the curated memory layer. remember(content, type, source, tags, confidence) inserts into curated_memories and syncs to the FTS5 index. recall_curated(query, type, limit) searches via FTS5 BM25 ranking. Results include the BM25 rank score. Soft delete and superseded memories excluded from search by default.
acceptance_criteria: remember('Dan prefers SQLite over Postgres', type='preference', source='manual') returns an integer ID
recall_curated('database preference') returns the stored memory with a rank score
recall_curated with type filter only returns memories of that type
recall_curated with limit=1 returns exactly 1 result
Soft-deleted memories (deleted_at set) do not appear in recall results
FTS5 search handles partial word matches via prefix queries
Results are returned as dicts with keys: id, content, type, source, tags, confidence, rank, created_at

features.name: Embedding engine with fastembed
features.description: Create embeddings module wrapping fastembed with bge-small-en-v1.5. Provide embed_one(text) -> list[float] and embed_batch(texts) -> list[list[float]]. Model loaded lazily on first call. Include serialize/deserialize helpers for SQLite BLOB storage. No PyTorch dependency.
acceptance_criteria: embed_one('hello world') returns a list of 384 floats
embed_batch(['a', 'b', 'c']) returns 3 vectors, each 384 floats
Round-tripping through serialize/deserialize produces identical vectors
Model is NOT loaded until the first embed call (lazy init)
No PyTorch in dependencies — only fastembed + onnxruntime

features.name: Raw layer: chunk storage and vector search
features.description: Implement the raw chunks layer. store_chunk(content, source, source_type, metadata) embeds the content, stores in raw_chunks with embedding BLOB, and indexes in sqlite-vec virtual table. recall_raw(query, source_type, limit) embeds the query, performs cosine similarity search via sqlite-vec, and returns ranked results.
acceptance_criteria: store_chunk stores content with embedding and returns an ID
recall_raw('database preference') finds semantically similar chunks
recall_raw with source_type filter only returns matching source types
recall_raw respects the limit parameter
Semantic search works: store 'I love pizza', search 'favorite food' returns it
sqlite-vec extension loads on the current platform without errors

features.name: Unified recall with layer fallback
features.description: Implement the unified recall(query, type, limit, layer) function. layer='curated' (default) searches FTS5 only. layer='raw' searches vectors only. layer='both' searches curated first, then fills remaining limit from raw, deduplicating.
acceptance_criteria: recall(query) defaults to curated layer search
recall(query, layer='raw') searches only the raw layer
recall(query, layer='both') returns results from both layers, curated first
layer='both' deduplicates results that exist in both layers
Results from both layers have a normalized score for consistent ranking
The simple query expansion splits 'database preference' into multiple search terms

features.name: Forget and supersede operations
features.description: Implement forget(id) for soft-delete and supersede(old_id, new_content) which creates a new curated memory with supersedes_id pointing to old_id. Supersession chains queryable via get_history(id).
acceptance_criteria: forget(id) sets deleted_at and memory no longer appears in recall results
forget does NOT physically delete the row
supersede(old_id, 'new fact') creates a new memory and soft-deletes the old one
The new memory's supersedes_id points to old_id
get_history(new_id) returns the full supersession chain including the old memory
Attempting to forget a non-existent ID raises a clear error

features.name: Confidence decay system
features.description: Implement confidence decay for curated memories. Exponential decay with configurable half-life. Access reinforces confidence.
acceptance_criteria: New memories start with confidence=1.0
Accessing a memory via recall reinforces it (confidence reset to 1.0, updated_at refreshed)
decay_confidence() reduces confidence based on time since updated_at
A memory untouched for 90 days has confidence ~0.5 (half-life)
A memory untouched for 180 days has confidence ~0.25
Memories with confidence < 0.1 are flagged as stale in status output

features.name: Status and health dashboard
features.description: Implement status() returning counts, type/source breakdowns, timestamps, db file size, stale count, integrity checks.
acceptance_criteria: status() returns a dict with keys: curated_count, raw_count, by_type, by_source, last_memory_at, last_consolidation_at, db_size_mb, stale_count
by_type maps memory types to counts
by_source maps source types to counts
stale_count is the number of curated memories with confidence < 0.1
Integrity check reports if FTS5 count differs from curated_memories count
Integrity check reports if vec index count differs from raw_chunks count

features.name: MCP server with 4 core tools
features.description: Create the MCP server using FastMCP. Expose exactly 4 tools: remember, recall, forget, status. Support stdio + SSE transport. CORTEX_DB_PATH env var.
acceptance_criteria: Running python -m cortex.server starts an MCP server on stdio transport
Running python -m cortex.server --transport sse --port 8765 starts SSE server
The server exposes exactly 4 tools: remember, recall, forget, status
remember tool accepts content, type, tags, layer parameters
recall tool accepts query, type, limit, layer parameters
forget tool accepts id and returns confirmation
status tool returns the full health dashboard
CORTEX_DB_PATH environment variable controls database location

features.name: Text chunking and raw layer ingestion pipeline
features.description: Create ingestion module for bulk content. chunk_text splits text into overlapping chunks. ingest_file reads, chunks, embeds, and stores. CLI entry point.
acceptance_criteria: chunk_text splits a 1000-word text into ~3 chunks of ~300 tokens each with overlap
ingest_file stores all chunks with embeddings in raw_chunks
Session log ingestion skips tool call messages and messages under 50 chars
CLI accepts a file path and source_type flag
Ingestion is idempotent — re-running on same file doesn't create duplicates
A summary is printed: N chunks ingested, M skipped

features.name: Extraction pipeline: raw to curated
features.description: Create extraction pipeline that promotes raw chunks to curated memories via Haiku prompts.
acceptance_criteria: extract_prompt generates a valid prompt listing raw chunks with IDs
The prompt instructs the LLM to output JSON with typed memories
process_extraction creates curated memories and extraction links
Each extraction links a raw_chunk_id to a curated_memory_id
CLI without --process outputs prompt to stdout
CLI with --process reads JSON from stdin and creates memories
Already-extracted chunks are skipped

features.name: MEMORY.md migration ingestion
features.description: One-time migration tool that reads existing MEMORY.md files and imports entries into curated layer.
acceptance_criteria: Running on Dan's actual MEMORY.md imports all entries as curated memories
Project entries get type='entity', User entries get type='preference', Feedback entries get type='procedure', Reference entries get type='fact'
Source is set to 'memory_md_migration' for all entries
Idempotent — running twice doesn't create duplicates
A summary is printed: N memories imported, M skipped (duplicates)

features.name: End-to-end test suite
features.description: Comprehensive pytest suite covering both layers and all operations.
acceptance_criteria: pytest runs all tests with 0 failures
Tests cover: remember, recall_curated, recall_raw, recall (unified), forget, supersede, get_history, decay_confidence, status
At least one test verifies FTS5 search ranks relevant results above irrelevant ones (30+ memories)
At least one test verifies vector search ranks semantically similar content higher (20+ chunks)
Tests use temporary databases (no leftover files)
Confidence decay math is tested: half-life produces expected values
MCP server can be instantiated without errors in tests

features.name: Graceful degradation and production hardening
features.description: Add graceful degradation if sqlite-vec/fastembed unavailable. Logging. CLI dispatcher. Error handling.
acceptance_criteria: If sqlite-vec is unavailable, curated layer still works and recall(layer='raw') returns a clear error message instead of crashing
If fastembed is unavailable, recall falls back to FTS5-only search with a warning
CORTEX_LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG enables verbose logging
CLI python -m cortex dispatches to subcommands: server, ingest, extract, migrate, status
MCP server returns structured error responses for invalid inputs (doesn't crash)
Running python -m cortex --version prints the version
